skill.name: kling-3-0
skill.description: Kling 3.0 video generation on RunComfy. Kling 3.0 (also called Kling V3.0) is Kuaishou Technology's third-generation multi-shot video model with native synchronized audio and consistent character identity across shots. This skill covers all six Kling 3.0 endpoints, spanning three rendering tiers (Standard, Pro, 4K) and two modes (text-to-video, image-to-video). Calls runcomfy run kling/kling-3.0/<tier>/<mode> through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "kling", "kling 3.0", "kling v3", "kling pro", "kling 4k", "kling text to video", "kling image to video", or any explicit ask to generate or animate with Kling 3.0.
skill.summary: Generate cinematic AI videos with Kling 3.0 across six endpoints on RunComfy, from Standard 1080p drafts to native 4K masters with synchronized audio.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer detected 99 pattern matches across external_commands, network, filesystem, and weak-crypto categories with a risk score of 100/100. After contextual evaluation, all 99 findings are confirmed false positives. The SKILL.md file is a markdown documentation file containing bash code block examples of the RunComfy CLI, legitimate API endpoint URLs, placeholder paths, and company names containing substrings that matched security patterns. No malicious code, no prompt injection, no data exfiltration, no credential theft. The skill is a legitimate wrapper for a commercial video generation API service.

low_findings.title: Shell backtick execution in markdown documentation (68 false positives)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 68 lines as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All occurrences are inside markdown-fenced bash code blocks documenting runcomfy CLI usage. These are documentation examples showing users how to invoke the CLI, not executable code. The backtick characters are markdown code fence delimiters (```bash), not shell execution operators.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in API documentation (15 false positives)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 15 lines as hardcoded URLs. These are legitimate RunComfy API endpoint documentation (runcomfy.com), documentation links, placeholder image URLs in CLI examples (https://.../source.jpg), and service reference links. All URLs point to the documented service's own domains or are explicit placeholders. No data exfiltration or unauthorized network access.

low_findings.title: Path traversal and hidden file access in documentation (5 false positives)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged lines with <absolute/path> placeholders in CLI examples as path traversal, and ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json as hidden file access. The <absolute/path> placeholder is standard CLI documentation convention indicating users must provide an output directory path. The token.json reference documents the legitimate RunComfy CLI token storage location with secure 0600 permissions. No malicious filesystem access.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm substring false match (11 false positives)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 11 lines as weak cryptographic algorithm usage. Zero cryptographic code exists in this file. The detection was triggered by the substring 'sha' within the company name 'Kuaishou' appearing in descriptive text. Other lines contain no hash functions, encryption, or cryptographic operations of any kind. This is a pure pattern-matching false positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell backtick/command execution patterns in markdown code blocks
dangerous_patterns.description: 68 locations where markdown code fences (```bash) were misidentified as shell backtick execution operators. All occurrences are within documented CLI usage examples. The skill is a wrapper that instructs an AI agent to construct and execute runcomfy CLI commands against a commercial API. No hidden or obfuscated command execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded URLs pointing to documented service endpoints
dangerous_patterns.description: 15 URLs detected as hardcoded. All point to runcomfy.com (the documented service) or are explicit placeholder URLs (https://.../source.jpg) in CLI examples. The skill documents a specific commercial API; the URLs are the service's own documented endpoints, not exfiltration targets.

dangerous_patterns.title: Path traversal sequences in CLI placeholder text
dangerous_patterns.description: <absolute/path> placeholders in 3 CLI example commands flagged as path traversal. These are documentation conventions indicating the user must supply an output directory path. The skill instructs the agent to write generated video files to a user-specified directory, which is the intended function.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm detection via substring match on company name
dangerous_patterns.description: 11 lines flagged for weak cryptographic algorithms. The detection was caused by the substring 'sha' in 'Kuaishou' (the company name). No hash functions, encryption, or cryptographic operations exist anywhere in this file. This is a textbook false positive from naive substring matching.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden file access in token storage documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json flagged as hidden file access. This is documentation of the standard token storage path used by the RunComfy CLI, stored with mode 0600 permissions. The security section explicitly documents this for transparency.

content.user_title: Generate cinematic AI videos with Kling 3.0 on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Kling 3.0 produces multi-shot cinematic video with synchronized audio and consistent characters across scenes. This skill connects Claude Code to six Kling 3.0 endpoints on RunComfy, from affordable Standard 1080p drafts to native 4K masters with audio.
actual_capabilities: Generate multi-shot cinematic video from text descriptions using six Kling 3.0 endpoints across three quality tiers
Animate still images into video with identity-preserving motion and controlled frame transitions
Produce synchronized native audio including dialogue, ambience, and music in a single generation pass
Render output from 1080p Standard drafts up to native 3840x2160 4K resolution
Control six aspect ratios including 16:9, 9:16 vertical, 1:1 square, and 21:9 wide cinema
limitations: Maximum 15 seconds per video generation across all Kling 3.0 tiers
Image-to-video mode requires a publicly accessible HTTPS image URL; local files are not supported
Maximum 6 continuous shots in a single Kling 3.0 4K generation
4K output files are very large; plan disk space and bandwidth before batch rendering

use_cases.title: Social media and short-form video production
use_cases.description: Generate vertical shorts, ads, and branded social content at 1080p using Kling 3.0 Standard and Pro tiers. Iterate quickly with cheap Standard drafts, then render finals on Pro for hero-quality motion.
use_cases.target_user: Content creator or social media manager

use_cases.title: Cinematic pre-visualization and storyboarding
use_cases.description: Create multi-shot cinematic sequences with synchronized audio at 4K for film pre-vis, pitch decks, and client presentations. Use multi-segment prompts to describe scene-by-scene transitions with consistent characters.

use_cases.title: Product and brand asset animation
use_cases.description: Animate product photos, brand assets, and character art into video using Kling 3.0 image-to-video mode. Supply a reference image and motion description to generate identity-stable animations with controlled camera movement.

prompt_templates.title: Simple text-to-video generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5-second 16:9 video of waves crashing on a rocky beach at golden hour using Kling 3.0 Standard.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick draft or preview render at the lowest cost tier

prompt_templates.title: Image-to-video with audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this product photo into an 8-second video. Use a slow camera push-in with subtle parallax. Enable synchronized audio with warm ambient background music. The image URL is https://example.com/product.jpg. Use Kling 3.0 Pro.
prompt_templates.scenario: Animating a brand asset or product photo with professional audio

prompt_templates.title: Multi-shot cinematic sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 12-second cinematic sequence in Kling 3.0 Pro. Shot 1: wide aerial of a mountain village at dawn, slow descent. Shot 2: medium tracking shot following a hiker on a trail. Shot 3: close-up of boots on rocky ground. Enable audio with natural mountain ambience and soft orchestral score.
prompt_templates.scenario: Multi-scene brand film or short narrative with consistent visual identity

prompt_templates.title: Controlled image-to-video with tail frame
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this character portrait in Kling 3.0 4K. Start from https://example.com/start.jpg and end at https://example.com/end.jpg. The character walks forward through a forest path over 10 seconds. Camera tracks backward. Enable audio with footsteps and forest atmosphere. 21:9 aspect ratio.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced controlled animation with locked start and end frames at 4K resolution

output_examples.input: Generate a 5-second 16:9 video of a coffee pour-over with morning sunlight streaming through a kitchen window, using Kling 3.0 Standard
output_examples.output: Kling 3.0 renders a 1080p clip with realistic steam rising from the coffee dripper, warm golden-hour light rays, and subtle condensation on the glass carafe. The motion is smooth and the lighting remains consistent throughout.

output_examples.input: Animate this portrait photo into an 8-second video with a subtle breathing effect and gentle camera dolly-in, using Kling 3.0 Pro with audio
output_examples.output: Kling 3.0 produces a 1080p video where the subject's chest rises and falls naturally, facial features remain identity-stable, and the camera pushes in slowly. The synchronized audio track includes soft ambient room tone with no dialogue.

output_examples.input: Create a 10-second 4K cinematic sequence of a spacecraft approaching a ringed planet, with three shots and orchestral audio, using Kling 3.0 4K
output_examples.output: Kling 3.0 renders a native 3840x2160 video with three identity-consistent shots: a wide establishing view of the planet, a medium shot of the spacecraft engines glowing, and a tight close-up of the cockpit instruments. The synchronized orchestral score swells across the shot transitions.

best_practices: Test prompts on Kling 3.0 Standard tier first before scaling to Pro or 4K to manage rendering costs
Lead prompts with camera and motion language such as wide shot, tracking shot, or slow push-in for best results
Use numbered shot descriptions like Shot 1:, Shot 2: when creating multi-scene sequences in a single Kling 3.0 generation
anti_patterns: Mixing conflicting style cues in one prompt such as photorealism and cartoon which confuses the model output
Requesting more than 15 seconds of video in a single generation call instead of segmenting into multiple renders
Using aspect ratios outside the supported set of 16:9, 9:16, 1:1, 4:3, 3:4, and 21:9 which causes rejection

faq.question: What is the maximum video length Kling 3.0 can generate?
faq.answer: Kling 3.0 supports up to 15 seconds per generation across all three tiers. For longer videos, segment your script into multiple generations and stitch them together.

faq.question: How does Kling 3.0 pricing work across the different tiers?
faq.answer: Standard tier starts at $0.084 per second without audio. Pro tier is $0.112 per second without audio. 4K tier is a flat $0.42 per second regardless of audio. Standard and Pro add about 50% when audio is enabled.

faq.question: Can Kling 3.0 use my local image files for image-to-video?
faq.answer: No. Kling 3.0 image-to-video requires a publicly accessible HTTPS URL for the source image. Upload your image to a public host or cloud storage first, then pass the URL.

faq.question: Does Kling 3.0 support generating audio alongside video?
faq.answer: Yes. Set generate_audio to true in your input. Kling 3.0 produces synchronized dialogue, ambient sound, and music in the same generation pass as the video.

faq.question: What aspect ratios does Kling 3.0 support?
faq.answer: Kling 3.0 supports six aspect ratios: 16:9 standard widescreen, 9:16 vertical, 1:1 square, 4:3 classic, 3:4 portrait, and 21:9 wide cinema. Other ratios are rejected or cropped.

faq.question: How is Kling 3.0 different from Kling 2.x?
faq.answer: Kling 3.0 adds stronger multi-shot identity preservation, longer maximum duration of 15 seconds, native 4K output on the 4K tier, and unified multi-prompt segment input that lets one generation contain several distinct scenes.
